Omaha, NE 68178

citations.narrative:
On January 20, 2026, rabbit 3-3 was euthanized due to injuries it received when it was able to enter the neighboring
enclosure of rabbit 3-4 and get in a fight. The wire enclosures have a door between them that is closed with a latch and
locked down with a metal clip so that the rabbits cannot lift the latch. There are two tabs protruding from the door latch
hardware that are about an inch apart where the metal clip can go. Placing the metal clip in one tab locks the latch in the
closed position, while the other tab can lock the latch in an open position and does nothing when the latch is closed.
The clip was accidentally put in the open position tab when the door was latched closed. During the night, the rabbit was
able to get the unsecured latch open and enter the enclosure of the neighboring rabbit. The rabbits in the study are post-
pubertal intact males who can see and interact with each across the wire walls that separate their enclosures and but
could be aggressive towards each other if they were not housed individually. When the caretaker arrived in the morning,
the door between the enclosures was open and both rabbits were in the same enclosure. There was dried blood and tufts
of fur on the floor of the enclosures. Physical exams were performed on both rabbits. Rabbit 3-4 had no wounds and just
a bit of blood on hair tips near its tail which was presumed to be from rabbit 3-3. Rabbit 3-3 was anesthetized to evaluate
its wounds. Upon examination, the attending veterinarian determined that rabbit 3-3 had a grave prognosis due to the
nature of its wounds and it was euthanized. Care must always be taken to ensure animals are handled as carefully as
possible to ensure their safety and prevent behavioral stress and physical trauma. Corrections to address the situation
had been made by time of inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian.
n

narrative:
2.38(f)(1)  Critical
   
Miscellaneous. 
On January 20, 2026, rabbit 3-3 was euthanized due to injuries it received when it was able to enter the neighboring 
enclosure of rabbit 3-4 and get in a fight. The wire enclosures have a door between them that is closed with a latch and 
locked down with a metal clip so that the rabbits cannot lift the latch. There are two tabs protruding from the door latch 
hardware that are about an inch apart where the metal clip can go. Placing the metal clip in one tab locks the latch in the 
closed position, while the other tab can lock the latch in an open position and does nothing when the latch is closed.  
The clip was accidentally put in the open position tab when the door was latched closed. During the night, the rabbit was 
able to get the unsecured latch open and enter the enclosure of the neighboring rabbit. The rabbits in the study are post-
pubertal intact males who can see and interact with each across the wire walls that separate their enclosures and but 
could be aggressive towards each other if they were not housed individually. When the caretaker arrived in the morning, 
the door between the enclosures was open and both rabbits were in the same enclosure. There was dried blood and tufts 
of fur on the floor of the enclosures. Physical exams were performed on both rabbits. Rabbit 3-4 had no wounds and just 
a bit of blood on hair tips near its tail which was presumed to be from rabbit 3-3. Rabbit 3-3 was anesthetized to evaluate 
its wounds. Upon examination, the attending veterinarian determined that rabbit 3-3 had a grave prognosis due to the 
nature of its wounds and it was euthanized. Care must always be taken to ensure animals are handled as carefully as 
possible to ensure their safety and prevent behavioral stress and physical trauma. Corrections to address the situation 
had been made by time of inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the attending veterinarian. 
 
 n
